entry.title: Re: Error activating Business Content Sales Analysis in S...
entry.content: seems exactly as the error with 1.2 version :). Are you sure, you downloaded the 1.3 version?

entry.title: Simple CAP Application Deploy BTP (Trial) : Found no compatible cell
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was having issues deploying my CAP application (complex) to BTP, running into a deploy issue:

Error staging application "xxx": NoCompatibleCell - Found no compatible cell

So I started from scratch with a simple application: 

- Install a fresh Trial Account

- Create a HANA DB (mapping to CF dev space)

- Create a simple CAP application in BAS, single table, simple service to display table entries

- Add hana (for production), Add mta

- Create MTA file

- Deploy to CF

All vanilla steps, explained in many blogs and CAPIRE. Nothing special, no manipulation of any package.json or any other files. 

However, no matter what I do. I always run into a CF deploy issue in the staging step stating (dmol extract) :

 
Failed to stage build: found no compatible cell with placement tag "trial" (STDERR, API)

I can't find any valuable information on this error, other than some non SAP related blogs mentioning that the stacks are not available on CF. As I did a clean install on a Trial account I assume the BTP environment is installed correctly. 

Would be great if somebody could provide some hints as I'm not able to deploy any application to BTP.

Regards,

Tom

entry.title: Re: Required Roles for SAP BW consultant to Work on SAP ECC
entry.content: Thank you, Roland. This has been really helpful to me.

entry.title: Re: Bom Explosion report Issue
entry.content:
Hi Ryan Crosby....

Yes STLAL  is one of the input parameter. If input  STLAL  parameter as '1' while entering  FG code , then the sub level components will be picked  with valie 1 by this function module  using STLAL  = 1 ?     suppose the sub level components  say '25001' has two values of stlal 1 and 2 .  Is there any provision to pick the stlal value 2 .

Actulay  i am working on an object in which FG is entered ( say having stlal value 1 and some sublevel components having stlal value 1  and other having stlal value 2 the latest )   i want to pick the report based on latest stlal value..

Regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
First of all, thank you for the "ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022"!

I was able to successfully install and login to S/4 system via SAP GUI as well as Eclipse with ADT and BWMT.

Is there any way to login to the backend database of this S/4 (DB Host: vhcala4hci) via Eclipse HANA modelling tools? I want to try out a hybrid scenario where one can consume a HANA view into BW modelling for reporting.

Regards,

Mandar Damle

entry.title: Re: Goods movement bin not determined or entered-Message...
entry.content:
I also had the same issue.
I did all the configuration related to Warehouse Process Type.


1. Define Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway

2. Assign Storage Types to Storage Type Search Sequence

3. Define Putaway Control Indicator

4. Specify Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway

5. Define Warehouse Process Type

6. Define Control Indicators for Determining Warehouse Process Types

7. Determine Warehouse Process Type

Even after that had the same problem.

Then i realized that i had not create a Storage bin for 9010 storage type to receive the goods while Unloading.

then i manually created storage bin GR-ZONE using the tcode /n/SCWM/LS01.

Then the Unloading issue solved.

 

Regards

Naeeum

entry.title: Re: EWM SMQ2--Error during mapping: material XXXXXXX does...
entry.content:
I also had the issue while creating Inbound delivery.
When i checked the log in SLG1 then the error was ' Could not determine the EWM data for: Availability Group XXXX 001 FF N'


Then maintained the customizations .
Path: SCM Extended Warehouse Management--Extended Warehouse Management--Goods Receipt Process--Configure Availability Group for Putaway

Completed all the steps.

 

Regards
Naeeum

entry.title: Re: Basic Start Date of Preventive Maintenance Order keep...
entry.content:
Hi Dan, Thank you for your time. Please see the details of the Maintenance Plan and the Maintenance Strategy in the plan.

I also thought it may be related to the measurement documents for the equipment but when I checked some of the WOs that were not rescheduled, the equipments did not have measurement documents as well, similar to the equipment associated with the WO I posted originally.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Migrating from CR9 nowadays
entry.content:
Hello,

The problem is the platform support for your app in VS 2022 and CR 9's database support.

See this for more info:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

And the Platform support info:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

CR for VS latest SP 36 supports Windows Framework 4.7 and 4.8. That VC 2005 runtime I believe is 1.x Framework.

The problem is CR has always been linked to Microsoft, so if MS end of life any of their products CR follows suit.

So to be Windows Compliant you must keep your app up to date.

I wrote a blog on upgrading to current versions:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

Sorry it's C# but the same upgrade info relates to VB.

You don't have to embed your reports into the app. First link has a few sample app's to show you how to load the unmanaged reports from a folder.

As for the main potential issue you may run into is the DB source you are using, ODBC, OLE DB, Native client etc.

CR embeds the DB client used to connect into the RPT file, so when you load the report it knows what Client to look for.

Depending on your data source depends on if you could have issues or not, CR dynamically loads the DB info on first load, open a report in CR 2020, 64 bit version and then Database, Set Location and connect to your data source.

Click on Verify Data Database, if any messages or database mapping UI pop's up then you have no option but to update each report manually. Unfortunately the DSK is not capable of updating the Data source, it can do it at run time but the updated info will not be saved in the Report.

So I can't say what possible issues you may have without more details.

All I can suggest is upgrade your app to VS 2022 using the 64 bit installer:

CR for Visual Studio SP36 64b installer (VS 2022 and above)

And set your Framework to 4.7 or 4.8, only one supported now.

Run your app and see what happens.... Note also you should set the Platform to x64, don't use AnyCPU, it can cause install problems on users PC's. CR uses the App Platform and not the PC's platform, although not much of an issue anymore since most users us 64 bit Windows, 32 bit OS's are no longer supported.

If you do need to update your reports get CR 2020 and use it to do the updating, if there are that many reports hire a student to do it for you....

You can get CR 2020 here:

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/crystal-reports.html#try-now

Note also that because CR 2020 is 64 bit your data source will need to support 64 bit Client.

Hope that helps?

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 : Auto resize ?
entry.content:
What you are experiencing is normal behaviour.

By tabbing to the height CR assumes you want to retain the same ratio.

If you don't want to change it then click OK without tabbing.

This is by design and normal behaviour.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAP...
entry.content:
Consider CDS views. Extractors are obsolete 🙂

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/5418de55938d1d22e10000000a44147b.html --> S/4 CDS views for extraction

Check OSS 2500202 as well. 

I also found a nice blog while searching: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/cds-views-vs-lis-in-pm-how-to-use-cds-views-to-create-your-own-queries/ba-p/13445774

Cheers

Martin

entry.title: Re: Bom Explosion report Issue
entry.content: @RKVASH any references to sub BOMs within a FG BOM is part of the master data, so the system will explode whichever alternative BOM is assigned as a subcomponent.

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 : The document could not be opened
entry.content:
Hello,

The likely cause is something has changed/been updated in the RPT file itself that CR 2020 doesn't know how to resolve.

This can be caused by the differences between versions, R&D does update the internal structure of the RPT file from time to time, expanding area's with enhanced details etc.

When opening a report there is an import routine that happens each time, the EXE reads the history and depending on what info it gets depend on what it does.

For example here's the SDK reading the history of the report:

What you are doing is updating that history when saving in CR 2020, then opening in CR 2013 and resaving it.

It is "cleaning" up the RPT file so it's import routine knows what to do with it now in CR 2020.

FYI - here's a KBA on this error:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2914156

If you want send me a link to download one of your reports, I'll have a look at it and see if I can duplicate the issue, if I can I'll pass it onto SAP R&D to have a look at it. And open it in my main test app and do some checks to see if it catches anything.

Do your reports have Saved data when you save them? It could be Database Client related possibly.

When you do open the reports in CR 2020 Click on Database menu, Verify Database and then save the reports, that may fix the issue. Verify Database does more than just the database info, it does check a few other things as well.

What is your data source cleint, ODBC, OLE DB, JDBC, Etc.?

Unfortunately there is no sure way to "fix" this, especially when it seems to be random.

entry.title: B1IF: Call POST http in json format with error
entry.content:
Hi There,

I would like to perform an http call in json format to call an API that is exposed.
And it work fine in Postman.
See screenshot:

And I refer to this post create a B1if scenario
[B1if: How to call PUT Http in json format](https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/b1if-how-to-call-put-http-in-json-format/qaq-p/11585506)
The step is, see attachment (step.xml).

And I got the below error:

<Payload Role="C" id="atom1" statusNo="HTTP400" statusMsg="Bad Request" reference="atom3" sysid="" payload="atom3" throwException="false">
<http.header/>
<httaError xmlns="urn:com.sap.b1i.xcellerator:upltdoc" HTTPcode="400" HTTPmsg="Bad Request">
<exceptionMsg>com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException: XCE212 Error while performing HTTP call; HTTP status is 400, Bad Request</exceptionMsg>
<returnData><!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01//EN""http://www.w3.org/TR/html4/strict.dtd"> <HTML><HEAD><TITLE>Bad Request</TITLE> <META HTTP-EQUIV="Content-Type" Content="text/html; charset=us-ascii"></HEAD> <BODY><h2>Bad Request - Invalid Header</h2> <hr><p>HTTP Error 400. The request has an invalid header name.</p> </BODY></HTML> </returnData>
</httaError>
</Payload>

 

Any suggestion?

BR

Robin

entry.title: Re: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
In BW you install content, you enhance it (like 0BPARTNER) and then if you re-install, while for example there is a change in the content you would like to implement, you changes will be overwritten. You also get a warning when installing content in BW, rt? Have the option to choose. Once content was installed, it was never re-installed.

Contrary to DSP.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAP...
entry.content:
Thank you, Martin.

Currently, we are still on ECC and might take a few more years to transition to S/4HANA, where we can leverage CDS views instead of extractors. In the meantime, we are seeking a solution to continue utilizing SAPI extractors or any insights on SAP's plans for supporting delta functionality for extractors moving forward in replication flow.

entry.title: Re: SSAM client stop syncing after update to 2410
entry.content:
Hi Omar,

I had the same problems with our SSAM 2310. After installing the patch from the note 3538225 the problem was solved and the SSAM 2310 worked again as it should.

You can find help on how to apply a patch here:

How to apply metadata patch in SAP Service and Asset Manager 

Regards

Philip

entry.title: Scoring system for SAP Activate Project Manager exam
entry.content:
Hello,

What is the scoring system for SAP Activate Project Manager exam?

For partially correct answers in multiple-choice questions, do we get penalized for incorrect choices?

Example: if there are 3 correct answers for a 5 choices question, and we select only 2 correct and 1 incorrect, is the score 2 out of 5 or otherwise?

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Display the "header" column in the same row than "pos...
entry.content:
Basically you want the program to display an ALV grid and not this old hierarchy ALV ?

  • Activation of LOG_MM_OPTIMIZATIONS provide a BAdI to change display of some MM reports:MB5B for example but not sure fir MB5L (only performance for this one?) - check for your version, not required to test in a sandbox system as this BF can be deinstalled
  • So you have to Enhance or Copy and adapt the report if you want to change this behavior.

entry.title: Re: Selection text not getting deleted in report
entry.content: Did you delete the SELECT-OPTIONS in the program source and then activate the source?

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Tactical Sourcing: Maximizing Benefits from this Guided Buying Channel

entry.title: Re: Problem for creation of cost element category 31
entry.content: I faced the same problem and find its solution. Change the field status of account group of Chart of account in Tcode OBD4. hide all the field status. The cost element category will not be hide. In Tcode FS00, you will see only cost element category. Assign the cost element category 31. Then system will allow to save secondary cost elements

entry.title: How to refresh the token for a OAuth 2.0 Client via Resource Owner Password Credentials
entry.content:
I have an OAuth 2.0 client set up in transaction OA2C_CONFIG. The grant type is Resource Owner Password Credentials. I am authenticating against this client from my ABAP code before calling the associated external API. But after a while, the token expires, and I would to request the user to reenter their user and password and set a new token.

From the UI, I would click the "Request Token" button in transaction OA2C_CONFIG (and enter user and password) to achieve this effect.

How do I do this in ABAP code?

I though I might do it like this

 

        CREATE OBJECT lo_oa2c_api
          EXPORTING
            i_client_uuid = lv_client_uuid.

        lo_oa2c_client->execute_ropc_flow(
          EXPORTING
            i_username = lv_username
            i_password = lv password
        ).

 

but SAP is checking via the callstack that the call is actually coming from the transaction OA2C_CONFIG and rejects the call if it was sent by another program. So I am clearly doing it wrong.

Can anyone advise what the right way would be?

I'm on S/4 2023 FP0.

entry.title: Creation Large amount of Meter Reading Orders via ISU_S_METERREAD_CREATE | MSG EL422
entry.content:
Need to create a large amount of Meter Reading Orders (100k installation) got an issue on how to debug.
Seems like this amount of data is too much that debugger can't really be used here it just exits.

When processing single installation everything is okay.
Therefore, I need to debug when processing large amount of data. 

That's why the first question does anyone has an idea on how to debug such a large amount of data or
ways to process such amount of data. We are looping through the 100k Installation and call 
ISU_S_METERREAD_CREATE

Second question during debugging or rather waiting on the debugger got the following message that stops the debugger.

EL422

An error occurred while uploading meter reading results via the IDoc of
type ISU_MR_UPLOAD or the BAPI MeterReadingDocument.Upload.
The system issues an error message and will not allow you to continue with this transaction until the error is resolved.

I don't get this because no IDOC is being used to my knowledge and these functions are not called when using ISU_S_METERREAD_CREATE. 

Looking at the next message mean looking at EL423 right but if the debugger just exit 
I can't debug it.

Thanks 

Best Regards,

ud39

entry.title: Re: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request
entry.content:
Hi @devangelgb ,

Could you provide some more details on how the external service definition looks like (e.g. edmx) how you imported it and what is the exact URL (or URL pattern) that doesn't work from the CAP side after external has been added, and how does the URL looks like in the working app / service?

Even better, could you provide an example of your code - if it contains confidental data, maybe you can create an isolated example with dummy data.

Regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: Migrating from CR9 nowadays
entry.content:
Hello Don

Thank you for answering to my question.

Below are some more info for my application:

My PC is a DELL Inspiron 3020 with Windows 11 Pro.

I am also using Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2022 (64-bit) - Current
Version 17.7.5  with NET Framework 4.7.2. for developing Windows VB.NET applications for any CPU target platforms.

Recently I successfully migrated my VB.NET 2005 projects with the help of Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2022 , NET Framework 4.7.2. Now all my applications can be recognized and operate in the new VS enviroment. I mean all the Windows forms communicate normally with the used datasourcses i.e. OLE DB Connections in conjunction with Microsoft SQL Server. Up to this point everything works perfectly and all the CRUD db operations behave ideally. This is a good success audit point and shows that the VB.NET apps work well in the development machine and also to the target machines with the help of the MS Setup deployment wizard.

The real headache is the reporting system. I am theoretically aware about the reasons that cause all the trouble in my reports. All my CR reports are designed with the CR 9 version with all the consequences and the impact to my projects. Business Objects has passed all the iknow how to knowledge to SAP which in turn means new SAP driver files etc. I must mention that there is no Technical Supporter/Represantative for SAP Crystal Reports in Greece where I live.

So which root do you think should I take so that these reporting problems will be vanished?

As I am not designing my reports into the VS enviroment, can I keep using the old Crystal Reports 9 application for designing new reports or updating existing ones?

Do I have to replace all the references of my applications to Crystal Decisions to anothers?

What CR restributables should I deploy to the target machines?

These are my questions for now.

I really appreciate your help.

Regards

Panagiotis Adam

entry.title: Re: Authorization for repl. model &1 and outbound impleme...
entry.content:
If the error is appearing only sometimes, and other times the identical data processes fine, then there are only two logical explanations:

  1. There are different receivers (e.g. if the receiver is an external program, there are registrations from different instances of that program, which are customized differently; or if the receiver is an ABAP system, there are different app servers that might be configured differently). The RFC layer then does load-balancing, and "sometimes" the tRFC LUW is sent to a receiver, where everything works, and "sometimes" it's sent to another receiver.
    Check the RFC destination closely in SM59, to see, whether there might indeed be substantially different receivers over which the LUWs are load-balanced...
  2. The LUW units are executed with different users. Sometimes a user, who has the necessary authorization in the target system, executes the call, and sometimes another user. (The error message at least indicates, that the target system complains about missing authorizations.)

entry.title: Re: Simple CAP Application Deploy BTP (Trial) : Found no compatible cell
entry.content: Can you share the mta.yaml file? I’m guessing that you are using somewhere trial as a plan for a service that doesn’t have trial as a plan. Besides that, can you als check the logs of the service while deploying? (Only if it is already available)

entry.title: Purchase Order Accrual-Product Type Group Account Assignment issue
entry.content:
How to use Product Type Group in configuration activity Create and Edit Account Determination for purchase order accruals. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Accrual-Product Type Group Account Ass...
entry.content:
See F1 Help on field Product Type Group

Product Type Group

Indicates the type of products that can be procured, for example, materials and services.

Use

In the product type group you choose to either procure a Material (by entering blank or 1) or a Service (by entering 2).

The procurement process is different for materials and services. For example, you can only maintain the field Service Performer when you purchase a service.

You also use the product type group Service to control that a service entry sheet can be created as a follow-on document of a purchase order.

The selection field Product Type Group is available for the following item categories:

  • Standard
  • Third-party
  • Limit

Dependencies

In the product master data, the assigned material type defines the product type group, that is, whether the product is a material or a service. This means that whenever you select, for example, Service as the product type group in your purchasing document item, you can only enter the number of a product with the same product type group in the Material field of the item.

And the other way round: if you enter a product number in the Material field, for example, in a purchase order item, the product type group is derived automatically from the material type assigned to the product master. In this case, you cannot manually maintain the product type group in the purchase order item.

If you don´t enter a product number, but create a free-text item or a limit item, you have to maintain the product type group manually.

entry.title: how to provide dynamic values in mta.yaml for dev and prod
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Below is the sample mta.yaml. I am using different db name for prod and dev.  Also i am using docker for srv module. How do we provide dynamic values for db and docker image. I am aware mta extension where we can change only the parameters. Any inputs are appreciated.
_____________________________________

  - name: test-srv
    type: application
    parameters:
      docker:
        image: testingname/testimage:latest
      memory: 1G
      disk-quota: 3G
    requires:
      - name:  test-uaa
      - name: test_dev_db

entry.title: how to trigger CAP side effects in synchronous call
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

How do we trigger side effects when we run the action in CAP fiori elements list page.

It works fine when i press the action and  functions are synchronous, But I am selecting multiple items and using synchronous where side effects are not working as the call is not waited for the function to complete. Any inputs are appreciated

entry.title: How to test a service SEGW
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I created a service in the segw following this blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/building-a-custom-sap-gateway-service-with-segw/ba-p/13616605

at point 6 of the blog, it does a test of the service by inserting request uri + /$format=XML

but how can I do a test of the service and to send the fields in input?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication...

SSO working just in the case of logging via SAP GUIweb?

Not working in the case of logging via standard SAP GUI app?

Thank you.

entry.title: Where can i find information, examples, about the template function?
entry.content: I need to mimin a state form exactly. Where can I find examples and instructions about how to use?

entry.title: Re: Simple CAP Application Deploy BTP (Trial) : Found no compatible cell
entry.content:
Hi Wouter,

Thanks for getting back to me.

Here is the extract of my mta.yaml file:

 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: bpm
version: 1.0.0
description: "A simple CAP project."
parameters:
enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
before-all:
- builder: custom
commands:
- npm ci
- npx cds build --production
modules:
- name: bpm-srv
type: nodejs
path: gen/srv
parameters:
buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
readiness-health-check-type: http
readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
build-parameters:
builder: npm
provides:
- name: srv-api # required by consumers of CAP services (e.g. approuter)
properties:
srv-url: ${default-url}
requires:
- name: bpm-auth
- name: bpm-db

- name: bpm
type: approuter.nodejs
path: app/router
parameters:
keep-existing-routes: true
disk-quota: 256M
memory: 256M
requires:
- name: srv-api
group: destinations
properties:
name: srv-api # must be used in xs-app.json as well
url: ~{srv-url}
forwardAuthToken: true
- name: bpm-auth

- name: bpm-db-deployer
type: hdb
path: gen/db
parameters:
buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
requires:
- name: bpm-db

resources:
- name: bpm-auth
type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
parameters:
service: xsuaa
service-plan: application
path: ./xs-security.json
config:
xsappname: bpm-${org}-${space}
tenant-mode: dedicated
- name: bpm-db
type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
parameters:
service: hana
service-plan: hdi-shared

Steps executed:

- Created a fresh BTP account

- Create a HANA Cloud service

- Open BAS

- cds init bpm --add tiny-sample

- cds add hana,xsuaa,approuter --for production

- cds add mta

- npm i

- mbt build -t gen --mtar mta.tar

- cf deploy gen/mta.tar

that's it, vanilla example however stuck during deploy. Extract dmol:

2024-11-23T23:59:10.97+0000 [API/6] OUT Uploading app package for app with guid 6a44f818-a9aa-44cd-940f-e971e551a499
2024-11-23T23:59:20.76+0000 [API/23] OUT Scaling process: "web"
2024-11-23T23:59:20.98+0000 [API/0] OUT Creating build for app with guid 6a44f818-a9aa-44cd-940f-e971e551a499
2024-11-24T00:00:38.62+0000 [API/7] ERR Failed to stage build: found no compatible cell with placement tag "trial"
2024-11-24T00:00:43.16+0000 [API/21] OUT Creating build for app with guid 6a44f818-a9aa-44cd-940f-e971e551a499
2024-11-24T00:02:09.47+0000 [API/19] ERR Failed to stage build: found no compatible cell with placement tag "trial"

Regards,

Tom

entry.title: Re: IAS for External Learner and Onboardee
entry.content:
Hi Sunny,

Here are key steps to help resolve the issue. If the issue persists, consider creating a case with SAP support for your IAS tenant to review further:

  1. Utilize a unique attribute (e.g., Employee ID or external learner ID) as the IAS username for both Learning and Onboarding profiles. Ensure this attribute remains consistent during user synchronization.
  2. Establish a clear governance process for managing profile transitions from external learner to employee. This may include:
    • Assigning temporary email addresses for onboarding profiles.
    • Automating profile merges post-onboarding to reduce administrative overhead.
  3. Review the integration architecture of IAS, Learning, and Onboarding to ensure no attribute conflicts occur during synchronization.

Hope this helps,

Thanks,

@Amin_Omidy

entry.title: MB52 'SQL code: 4' error
entry.content:
Hi  Experts

When I use MB52 for a full query and there is a error ' SQL error "SQL code: 4" occurred while accessing table "NSDM_E_MSPR".'  Can you help me .

entry.title: Re: How to test a service SEGW
entry.content:
Hi, Below are the solution for question.

Get operation: Select entity name as per your requirement in entity set and pass key fields input data in entity as below of sample sales order API in URI.

POST Operation:  Select entity name in entity set as per your requirement and prepare payload for request.

entry.title: Re: How to test a service SEGW
entry.content:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/demo-test-cases/ba-p/13265490

 

do google.

entry.title: Using Azure Private Virtual Network Endpoints with SAP Datasphere Replication
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere offers an ability to move SAP Data into Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (ADLS Gen2) by using Replication Flows. By default, the ADLS Gen2 Account needs to be exposed on the public network for it to be written to.

This blog is about the scenarios to restrict the Azure container (only accessible on a private vlan) and allow SAP Datasphere to write to it. 

As part of the setup, an SAP Datasphere instance running on BTP Subaccount using Azure is needed:

From the SAP Discovery Center, choose which one is best for your requirements. Note the SAP Datasphere roadmap which has upcoming additional Azure Datacenters:

Determine the approprite Region. In this example, I will use US West - WA

In my BTP Cockpit, I need to have access to a BTP sub account in this region. I don't currently have one, so creating one (Noting the Azure Region):

 Before you create the SAP Datasphere instance, you will need to add the Entitlements to the space:

Create the Datsphere instance:

While this creating, the ADLS Gen2 components needs to be created in the same region:

Before the Storage Account can be created, and Virtual Network is needed: 

 Then create the Storage Account:

 

 

Updating the Networking to allow access from selected virtual networks and IP Addresses. 

Below is using just the configured vnet to allow Datasphere to access the Storage Account:

 Once Datasphere is available, perform the following:

  1. Create a Space
  2. Create a User and associated scoped Role and assign them to the Space.

Then note the Virtual Network Subnet ID (Microsoft Azure) in the About menu.

Using the SAP Note as a reference the Virtual Network Subnet ID needs to be added via the cli to the Azure storage container. 

Sidenote:

If you need to Install the az cli on a mac:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/install-azure-cli-macos

az login (picking the right subscription)

The command required to add the SAP datasphere to the ADLS Gen 2 virtual subnet

 

az storage account network-rule add --resource-group ANZ-BTP-Sharepoint --account-name azureuswestwafordsp --subnet /subscriptions/e1873ac2-a3f3-48fc-8ce9-20724bb56803/resourceGroups/dis-i667u/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/dis-i667u/subnets/shoot--di-us20--dis-i667u-nodes

 

Note : Setting the "az interactive" worked for me as opposed to the default shell or bash.

  • Resource Group is located in the Azure Storage Container.
  • Account Name is the Storage Container Name.
  • Subnet is the Virtual Network Subnet ID

So using the Azure Endpoint: azureuswestwafordsp, and getting the Access Key:

There is other options to access the ADLS Gen2 container, but I have kept it simple. 

Back in SAP Datasphere, Create the Azure Connection:

 And testing the Connection:

Also since this a new SAP Datasphere instance, the Premium Outbound needs to be enabled:

Then SAVE and allow the Tenant to be reconfigured.

Normally I would use an S/4HANA source, but in the interest of time I have added a simple Local Table Source and creating a simple Replication Flow

Adding the Azure ADSL Gen2 as the target, initially I get the error:

An error occurred while fetching container data.

We can provide the following information to assist either you or an SAP engineer to solve the issue:
Correlation ID: c213c159-dcf9-4578-49ec-8a21f7140f49
HTTP Status: 500 error
Error Code: connectionbrowse
Technical Message: Failed to fetch the data. Error in getDisConnectionChildren. Failed to get container children for /. Cause 0: Operation failed: \x26#34;This endpoint does not support BlobStorageEvents or SoftDelete. Please disable these account features if you would like to use this endpoint.\x26#34;, 409, GET, https:\/\/azureuswestwafordsp.dfs.core.windows.net\/?resource=account\x26amp;maxResults=5000\x26amp;timeout=90, EndpointUnsupportedAccountFeatures, \x26#34;This endpoint does not support BlobStorageEvents or SoftDelete. Please disable these account features if you would like to use this endpoint. RequestId:794b7c11\-c01f\-0037\-0e57\-3dc067000000 Time:2024\-11\-23T03:25:28.7784520Z\x26#34; Code: 99999; Cause 1: Operation failed: \x26#34;This endpoint does not support BlobStorageEvents or SoftDelete. Please disable these account features if you would like to use this endpoint.\x26#34;, 409, GET, https:\/\/azureuswestwafordsp.dfs.core.windows.net\/?resource=account\x26amp;maxResults=5000\x26amp;timeout=90, EndpointUnsupportedAccountFeatures, \x26#34;This endpoint does not support BlobStorageEvents or SoftDelete. Please disable these account features if you would like to use this endpoint. RequestId:794b7c11\-c01f\-0037\-0e57\-3dc067000000 Time:2024\-11\-23T03:25:28.7784520Z\x26#34; Code: 99999; Cause 2: Could not browse resource hdlfs://storagegateway-agent:80// on HDFS connection. Code: 80015
 
Changing the defaul Azure storage account details:

 Trying again, I can access the Azure Storage Container:

And create the simple Replication Flow:

Save and Deploy the Replication Flow. Once the job completes:

Attempt to view the file in Azure:

After adding My Client IP address:

 And viewing the file in a parquet viewer:

The above process took only a couple of hours and it was creating everything from scratch. 

Also as test, using the same ADLS Gen2 storage connection on an AWS Account - when validating the connection:

 But when trying to view the details of the Container:

Correlation ID: 5d7cf07b-daee-408f-6c1f-04a3d6bf12b5
HTTP Status: 500 error
Error Code: connectionbrowse
Technical Message: Failed to fetch the data. Error in getDisConnectionChildren. Failed to get container children for /. Cause 0: Forbidden Code: 99999; Cause 1: Could not browse resource hdlfs://storagegateway-agent:80// on HDFS connection. Code: 80015

Which is what was expected. This ADLS Gen2 container didn't have any IP addresses in the allowlist in the firewall either.

In a simplier approach, you can also add the Replication Flow IP Address to the Storage Account Firewall - which can also limit access - if you are running the Datasphere instance on AWS or can't use the same region as outline above. Not as secure but a work around.

Also - the instances used above are only temporary and will be deleted - hence I haven't blurred the details.

Hope this helps others requiring this functionality. Please comment if I missed something or you need some help.

entry.title: Re: Enhanced Procurement Analytics with SAP Datasphere - KAUST POC Case Study
entry.content:
Hello @Sharaff,

Yes, we are utilizing the Data Extractor App for Procurement to pull data from SAP Ariba Cloud Services via standard APIs and store the relevant information in a database.To automate data extractions on a set schedule, we have configured the SAP BTP Job Scheduler service to trigger extraction jobs in the Data Extractor application through the available endpoints.

In SAP Datasphere cockpit, we have leveraged the pre-packaged business content via Semantic Onboarding.

For more detailed technical insights on the integration, please refer to the link below, as this blog focuses primarily on the Customer Proof of Concept.
SAP Discovery Center Mission - Leverage SAP Ariba Foundation model in SAP Datasphere (cloud.sap)

entry.title: Excel Macro for forcing EPM member selector to display ID or Description in EPM Reports SAP BPC
entry.content:
In EPM, Based on Context EPM Member selector Display options Selection, EPM Report will display dimensions in same format.

Like, If we maintain ID in member selector, relevant dimension will display IDs in the EPM Report.

Similarly, If we maintain Description in  member selector, EPM Report will display  Descriptions in EPM Report.

Irrespective Context member display settings selection, we have to display ID or Description through EXCEL Macro.

I have searched relevant FPMXL functions for this purpose, but could not find relevant function for this scenario.

Please suggest if we have possible macro or any other way we can achieve this requirement.

 

Best Regards,

Anil

entry.title: Re: Enhanced Procurement Analytics with SAP Datasphere - KAUST POC Case Study
entry.content: I appreciate the clarification. Thank you @lalitmohan

entry.title: Re: How to Add Extension Field in Supplier Invoice Upload Excel Template?
entry.content:
Hello fellow developers and tinkerers

I managed to use this blog to add some Field Extensions to the Opportunities Excel upload template, but the one thing that stopped it from working was the bit about the namespace that ends with 'ZZZZZ'. Well, make sure this is the correct codification of the namespace or else it won't work!

How do you find out the coding of the namespace? Just go into Adaptation Mode and look in "Further Usage" for one of your Field Extensions, then go to the Services tab. The text at the top tells you the exact namespace to use:

Hope that helps.

Tim

entry.title: How to know the utilization percentage for the implemented SAP modules?
entry.content:
Dears

I want to know the utilization percentage for the implemented SAP modules, the reason is to reach the optimum utilization of SAP capabilities in them. 

Best regards

entry.title: Test Cases of Descoped Solution Processes in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
This post is about test preparation in Cloud ALM. This covers my questions and views/suggestions about test preparations

  • I am trying to find if there is a better way of dealing with test cases whose solution process is descoped from the project scope. I see no indication of such test cases in the test preparations application. In the case of a dynamic project scope, Is there a better way to deal with such scenarios rather than using the filter/search? 

In my view, while descoping a process, changing the status of test cases to a new status  (something like "descoped") will give a chance to easily review such test cases. From the content and reusability perspective, I feel that process-oriented test case preparation will be better instead of project-oriented. This allows test cases to be used in multiple projects. Or, is it only me dreaming this to be as good as SolDoc 😃    

Looking forward to your view on this.

SAP Cloud ALM Implementation Methodologies

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA for Product Compliance - labelling follow up...
entry.content:
Hello

regarding labels...  i try to explain at my very best:

The "layout" and the "printing" is done not by SAP but with a partner solution. Only the data to print is shared.

I would assume: Barcode etc. will be supported by the partner solution.

I would assume to get the same capabilities with the new Label Solution as with the old solution. So it should be possible to determine non "EHS" like labels and print them

Regarding Access concept... sorry. no idea. This is a further learning path if you use "Product Compliance".

Wish you the best

C.B.

entry.title: Re: GLM / WWI grahics as vector vs. pixel
entry.content:
Hello

I am not aware of any limitation in Winword. And whatever Winword can use as a graphis is from my point of view supported. C.B.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA product Compliance - NEW Dangerous Goods Management Class 7 support
entry.content:
Hello

regarding

  1. It is unclear to me if this solution in S/4HANA 2023 actually generates a dangerous goods note as standard or not. The roadmap I reviewed suggests to me that the generation of dangerous goods notes is unavailable. Please confirm if my assumption is correct. If so, this would mean that we will require an enhancement to print a customised form.

I would assume: you get a "default" delivery form.. But never seen that somebody is using the SAP default

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA product Compliance - NEW Dangerous Goods Management Class 7 support
entry.content:
Hello regarding: Can the same material representing a packaged product be assigned to multiple different materials representing unpackaged products? We have a scenario where a parent material representing a container will have different child materials that represent different compositions and concentrations.

I would assume as in EHS classic: this is not allowed. The link of material to "CCI" (either packed or unpacked) must be unique

Normally a "container" is not handled as  material, but as a "packaging" to combine many material for the transport. PLease explain your scenario...

C.B.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA product Compliance - NEW Dangerous Goods Management Class 7 support
entry.content:
Regarding: The customer completes their classification of items on a batch-by-batch basis e.g. at delivery rather than at master data level. If that "packaged product" contains less quantity of the unpackaged material, then it may be classified under a different number. Further, it may have different enclosures depending on what unpackaged material is inside the packaged product. Finally, the dangerous goods note will have to have varied data on it depending on the material and qty in the packaged product. What would be the correct way of managing this?

In EHS Classic only under some conditions you can do a "classification" in the delivery. i would assume: This feature is on the roadmap of SAP but not available yet

The "delivery" wise DG classification is in EHS Classic foreseen for "one time" and "template" materials. But not in the normal process. Who do you have solved this in EHS Classic?

C.B

entry.title: Update huge data with CPI JDBC
entry.content:
We have database table that has more than 20 million records, and create a iflow job to run daily to SELECT all records check and do some pressing then UPDATE them.

Processing all of them once not working because JDBC taking so much time for SELECT query only.

 

So, I add loop to call 10,000 records in each iteration and UPDATE them . it is working but taking time, more than 7 hours to finish 500,000 records that means to finish all records could take more than one whole day.

What is the best solution to increase the performance?

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan with Completion Indicator CHECKED ge...
entry.content:
Hi @indraniljoshi1989,

Notice that you've activated enhancing call horizon functionality (switch -  EAM_SFWS_MPLAN_OPEN_HORIZ_DAYS) which allows you to set call horizon in days. This may be the cause for some plans bypassing the completion requirement check. 

Check this SAP note and implement the corrections - 1849123 - EAMCC: Enhancing the call horizon- check for call completion - SAP for Me

Regards,
Ritesh PR

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Screening
entry.content: Hi, I agree, looks like there isnt a standard field shown in the Fiori app for Screening Maintenance Requests, but if you drill into the notification (Change Maintenance Notification Web Dynpro app), you can get the details from the additional functions > Status Information menu path.

entry.title: Re: How to find a CDS view for a classic Table in ABAP? - Cheat Sheet
entry.content:
Hello @LBretschneider 
it seems that the Link to the excel has been expired. Can you provide it with a permanent link? 

BR

Christian

entry.title: Re: how to provide dynamic values in mta.yaml for dev and...
entry.content: If you use the BTP provided Database Options like HANA Cloud or PostgreSQL you simply would bind the DB instance to your application and evaluate the VCAP_SERVICES environment variable inside your container.

entry.title: Import ICMR for ECC Source Data
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We are evaluating implementing ICMR for an organization that has most of its interco activity in an ECC data source.
The plan to implement Group Reporting Public Cloud.
1 How do we extract the BSEG data ?

2 What are the common matching fields for AP AR and income/expense?
I understand it can vary but I also believe it’s reference file 1,2,4 (XREF1..) and in BELNR for the BKPF table. However BKPF is not part of the ICMR matching field choices.

3 Do customers typically reconcile at the Transaction (FI document level) or group currency level?
4 If ETL is required than do we? Or how do we? use GRDC? GRDC will import ECC to GR and ECC/BSEG fields aren’t relevant or available to GR?

Thanks in advance.

Eyal Feiler

entry.title: Error in FB75 while raising to E-Invoice
entry.content:
Dears,

I am facing an issue when I am posting an FI Credit Note using transaction FB75 while submitting to ZATCA (KSA E-Invoicing). The error message is: (Field ID is empty in Invoice Document Reference of Billing Reference) as showing below.
Any solution for that ?

entry.title: Profit center group and Dummy profit center is not visible in KCH5N
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created profit Center std. hierarchy, Dummy profit center, profit center groups and 6 profit centers. All of them were active(green) last night. But these are not visible today in KCH5N. I can see the profit centers through search button but profit center group does not pull any info getting a message " The search has not produced any hits". Noe sure what did I do wrong. Any help will be appreciated. Here is two screenshots

entry.title: SAP runs SAP: Insights into Financial Closing Transformation with SAP AFC
entry.content:
Financial Closing Transformation

As financial operations grow in complexity, organizations increasingly seek to optimize their financial closing processes. SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) has emerged as a robust solution to enhance these operations, bringing automation, streamlined task management, and greater transparency to the closing cycle. This blog delves into our transformation journey through SAP AFC, exploring key steps from initial template cleanup to advanced automation and integration efforts. The outcomes have not only improved efficiency but also empowered teams to focus on more strategic, value-adding activities.

FCC Template Cleanup: A Strategic Preparation for AFC Implementation

A crucial early step in our SAP AFC implementation journey was cleaning up templates of the predecessor tool – FCC (Financial Closing Cockpit), an often overlooked but vital task. Over time, tasks were added to the FCC, many of which became obsolete due to evolving processes, yet they remained active in the system. As a result, certain teams either never used these tasks, or they were inconsistently activated, causing confusion over the true status of the financial closing. This overload made it difficult for stakeholders to get a clear view of progress. Therefore, our initial focus was a thorough review of over 20,000 tasks in the FCC. Each unique task was assessed for its relevance, current use, and necessity. This meticulous review allowed us to filter out redundant tasks and focus on the critical ones, eliminating unnecessary clutter in the system and enhancing the overall closing process. Following the cleanup, we identified opportunities to replace or improve outdated automations. In many instances, tasks previously performed manually were transformed into fully automated processes. This initial streamlining laid the groundwork for more sophisticated automations and faster financial closing cycles, ensuring that only essential tasks entered the AFC framework.

Replacing Third-Party Automations with SAP iRPA: Efficiency and Cost Reduction

One of the significant challenges in modern financial departments is managing diverse automation tools. Before implementing AFC, we relied on third-party automation platforms, which, while functional, came with high operational costs and limited scalability. SAP’s native automation tool, SAP SBPA (SAP Build Process Automation, previously SAP intelligent Robotics Process Automation), proved to be a game-changer, offering deep integration with SAP environments, and reducing external dependencies. In a relatively short period, we developed, tested, and deployed multiple SAP iRPA bots dedicated to various financial processes. These bots now handle essential tasks such as bank reconciliation preparation and the analysis and posting of general bad debts. We currently operate eight unattended bots in SAP iRPA, each designed to execute specific financial operations autonomously. These bots have streamlined processes, eliminated manual intervention, and substantially reduced the time required to complete routine tasks. The shift from third-party to SAP automation not only simplified our tech stack but also led to measurable cost reductions by consolidating our tools within the SAP ecosystem.

Simplifying Authorization Management and SCIM Interface Integration

Effective role and authorization management is vital for maintaining security and operational efficiency, especially in complex financial environments. Prior to implementing SAP AFC, our authorization management processes, while functional, required streamlining to improve efficiency and ease of use. Roles were often unclear, and responsibilities were scattered across various departments, leading to inefficiencies in task execution and oversight. The integration of the SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) interface into SAP AFC introduced a new level of simplicity and precision to our authorization processes. This integration allowed for the automatic provisioning and de-provisioning of users based on their roles, making it easier to manage user access and control. The new role structure under AFC is much clearer and more effective, aligning specific tasks with precise static and instance authorizations such as end-user, support, or administrator. Additionally, scoped roles further refine access by limiting user permissions to specific areas, such as country-specific operations or department-specific tasks. This ensures that users only have access to what is necessary for their roles, reducing the risk of unauthorized access. This shift not only improved operational clarity but also enhanced security by reducing the risk of unauthorized access. The streamlined approach to authorization management also facilitates quicker onboarding for new team members and minimizes downtime related to role changes.

Task Structure and Management Optimization: Building for Efficiency

One of the standout features of SAP AFC is the ability to create Task List Models in the backend, which simplifies the administration and management of financial closing tasks. This capability allowed us to develop a new structure for task management, linking closing tasks to our financial closing timetable. The timetable is accessible as a high-level overview within AFC, giving managers and stakeholders an intuitive view of the entire closing process. As a result, task management has become significantly more organized and accessible. This task management system not only streamlines task administration but also ensures logical connections between tasks, making them easier to track. Through the "Manage Closing Task Lists" app, authorized users can oversee the closing structure, manage dependencies, and task parameters, and effortlessly generate new task lists. Monitoring task execution times and error rates has been another critical aspect of this transformation. By analysing this data, we can make real-time adjustments to the process, optimizing execution times and improving user experiences. This continuous improvement cycle has helped us minimize delays, address bottlenecks promptly, and ensure that our financial closing runs smoothly.

Expanding Automation: Continuous Collaboration and Innovation

Automation has been central to our AFC transformation, but our efforts didn’t stop at standard automations. By collaborating with different finance teams and other departments, we continuously identify new use cases for automation. This collaborative approach has allowed us to expand the scope of AFC beyond its original framework. One of the key benefits of AFC is its flexibility. We’ve used this to our advantage by enabling teams to easily review the results of automated tasks and monitor their progress. Rather than introducing new manual tasks, we have focused on extending automation to cover more scenarios. This has been particularly useful for high-volume, repetitive tasks, where automation reduces the risk of human error and accelerates completion. A key feature that has facilitated this expansion is the use of task dependencies, which allow for the automatic triggering of subsequent tasks once preceding tasks are completed. This has enabled us to create process chains that execute with minimal intervention, further reducing the burden on our teams.

Better Task Organization and Responsibility Distribution Across Finance Departments

A key improvement in our financial closing processes has been the enhanced organization and clarity of tasks. By adopting a standardized naming convention and utilizing custom fields, we can now easily identify which financial area each task pertains to. This improved organization has simplified task management across the department, making it easier to assign and track responsibilities. In the past, hundreds of users were involved in triggering and monitoring individual tasks, which required significant coordination. With AFC, we have shifted to a more centralized approach, where a smaller group of users is responsible for specific tasks. By using user groups, we have reduced the need for widespread user involvement, freeing up valuable resources to focus on higher-value activities. This simplification has been a game-changer, allowing us to concentrate on more strategic tasks rather than being bogged down by the execution of repetitive, low-value processes.

Significant Error Reduction and Faster Closing Cycle

One of the most significant results of our AFC implementation has been the dramatic reduction in error rates. By reviewing each task and continuously developing improvements, we’ve been able to eliminate many common issues that previously plagued the financial closing process. Today, manual tasks are primarily reserved for documentation, approval, or compliance-related checks, with most tasks now fully automated. This shift has not only improved accuracy but also shortened the overall financial closing cycle. A key feature supporting this transformation is the Financial Closing Overview and Closing Task completion apps within AFC, which provides real-time insights into the status of the closing process. Users can monitor both the general progress and individual tasks with ease, allowing them to make timely decisions and adjustments. Proper notification scenarios ensure that users are always informed about task status, further enhancing the efficiency of the closing process. The current automation rate in AFC-integrated systems is around 88%, indicating high operational efficiency. Additionally, the error rate for automated tasks has been significantly reduced and stabilized at approximately 5%, well within the acceptable failure criteria for optimal performance.

The Future of AFC: Expanding Automation with Group Reporting Integration and AI-Guided Error Resolution

Looking ahead, we are planning to integrate Group Reporting with AFC, marking a new chapter in our financial closing journey. This integration will bring even greater transparency and efficiency to our financial reporting processes, allowing us to further extend the scope of automation and process optimization. In addition to this, we are currently testing and improving our new solution for Gen AI-guided error resolution in AFC, which promises to significantly enhance the speed and accuracy of identifying and resolving issues.

In conclusion, SAP AFC has been instrumental in transforming our financial closing processes. By reducing manual tasks, enhancing automation, simplifying task management, and improving user experiences, we’ve not only streamlined operations but also positioned our teams to focus on strategic, value-adding activities. Our AFC journey is ongoing, and as we continue to innovate and expand its scope, including advancements in AI-driven solutions, we look forward to achieving even greater operational excellence in the future.

entry.title: Re: Maintain Assignment of Document Info Record to Maintenance Task Lists via API, OAuth and Postman
entry.content:
Hi Pascal,

 I tried the instruction for Scenario 2 and successfully uploaded an attachment. My additional question is, what is the process when I want the two attachments to be on two different document number because as I have tried uploading file again, the new file was uploaded to the initial document created.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: ODATA API ‘Attachments’ Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Prabha,

 I tried the instruction for Attach Document to Business Objects and successfully uploaded an attachment in the method using the binary. My additional question is, what is the process when I want the two attachments to be on two different document number because as I have tried uploading file again, the new file was uploaded to the initial document created.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI): Use cases of node functions in message mapping
entry.content: Excellent blog. Thank you! Very helpful that it is structured along scenarios / patterns.

entry.title: Re: SAP Data Sphere - Replication Flow
entry.content:
Dear Lokesh,

Thanks for sharing detailed information about Replication Flow. I have a query.

What is the expression to filter Null/ Blank rows from source table to Datasphere.  I have tried '', ' ', =IsNothing, Null, 'Null'. These are giving error. Any clue would be helpful

entry.title: secussl_read: SSL_read() failed
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are facing problem that SAP instance can not connect to webdispatcher  due to certificate issue, 

Refer to below log:

[Thr 140520558421760] Sun Nov 24 15:17:32:142 2024 [Thr 140520558421760] Server SSL_CTX 7fcd784a7500 pvflags=897 (TLSv1.2,TLSv1.1,TLSv1.0,BC) [Thr 140520558421760] secussl_read: SSL_read() failed (536875078/0x20001046) [Thr 140520558421760] =

entry.title: Re: Simple CAP Application Deploy BTP (Trial) : Found no compatible cell
entry.content:
I can confirm the issue:

  2024-11-24T10:29:26.33+0100 [API/20] ERR Failed to stage build: found no compatible cell with placement tag "trial"

when I try to deploy https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-sflight. It's a freshly created Trial acocunt in the Extension Landscape us10-002 (https://api.cf.us10-002.hana.ondemand.com). Seems someone forgot to do the needed setup for trial in this CF landscape.

The CF Coding is: https://github.com/cloudfoundry/auction/blob/main/auctiontypes/types.go#L27

Maybe @thomas_jung can trigger the right people?

entry.title: Re: Free goods in purch Info record MM SAP S/4
entry.content: Dear @Sanan, The blog it explaining free goods process in SD what I'm looking for isfree goods in MM and how to set rules in the info record so those rules will be inherited in the PO
